summary: Bundled stable Linux capture and NVENC encode evidence for downstream non-Linux decoder work. The bundle references upstream artifacts by repo-relative path, records SHA-256 hashes and byte sizes, validates sample-av1.ivf with ffprobe, and explicitly excludes unproven performance, zero-copy, Mac decode/render, HDR, and no-CPU-readback claims.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Evidence bundle includes capture metadata, encoded sample, hashes, logs, and notes.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: bundle-manifest.json references capture metadata, sample-av1.ivf, upstream and local logs, notes, reports, SHA-256 hashes, and byte sizes.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Sample artifact can be consumed by a non-Linux decoder later.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: bundle-manifest.json identifies evidence/m2-nvenc-encode-sample/sample-av1.ivf as the stable decoder-consumable sample; local ffprobe validates AV1 Main in IVF with AV01 tag.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: All unproven performance or zero-copy claims are explicitly absent or filed as findings.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: bundle-manifest.json and notes.md list explicit non-claims for latency, direct DMA-BUF import into NVENC, VideoToolbox decode, Metal render, HDR, no CPU readback after grim, and performance from low-latency tune.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: qd findings exist for any known gaps.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: qd findings eed2a910-d829-46e8-af64-837ed01ed78e, 28358c32-733e-4e8e-8f6b-f5c2d4d13c99, 77eec369-a6fe-4261-8dcf-1fbd8a575ca7, fcaaae62-2275-44ce-ae76-a99e45e06cb8, and a2842577-9a8b-4601-862b-0cddb671c832 track the known gaps.

commandsRun.evidence: terminal output; summarized in evidence/m2-video-path-validation/commands.log and bundle-manifest.json

commandsRun.command: sha256sum/stat over referenced upstream evidence and reports

realWorldValidation.evidence: ffprobe successfully parsed sample-av1.ivf in this node and upstream evidence records the Linux capture and NVENC encode runs.

trackedQdFindings.title: m2-video-path-validation does not prove direct DMA-BUF import into NVENC
trackedQdFindings.body: The stable sample was produced through a grim PNG/RGB capture and ffmpeg/NVENC process boundary with format=nv12. File this as a gap before any downstream work claims zero-copy Linux capture-to-encode.

trackedQdFindings.title: m2-video-path-validation does not prove downstream Mac decode/render
trackedQdFindings.body: sample-av1.ivf probes as AV1/IVF on Linux, but no VideoToolbox decode or Metal render has been executed yet. Downstream Mac work must validate that path before claiming it.

trackedQdFindings.title: m2-video-path-validation does not measure streaming latency or performance
trackedQdFindings.body: The bundle contains a one-frame artifact and command logs only. No end-to-end streaming latency, encode latency, throughput, or CPU/GPU utilization claims are proven.

trackedQdFindings.title: m2-video-path-validation does not validate HDR or color-management behavior
trackedQdFindings.body: ffprobe reports yuv420p with color_range=tv and unknown color_space/color_transfer/color_primaries. HDR handling remains unvalidated.

trackedQdFindings.title: m2-video-path-validation does not prove no CPU readback after grim capture
trackedQdFindings.body: The encode sample explicitly materializes captured-sample.png and captured-sample.rgb before NVENC encode. No zero-copy or no-CPU-readback claim should be made for the grim-based sample.
